notes: https://pomb.us/build-your-own-react/. Canonical React-from-scratch tutorial. Fiber, reconciliation, hooks. Directly applicable — React 19 in SoNash stack.
https://github.com/hexacta/didact. Virtual DOM diffing focus. Complementary to Pomber. Understanding re-render behavior.
https://wyag.thb.lt. Python git-from-scratch. Understanding object model helps design better hook/worktree infrastructure.

candidate: Regex Matching Can Be Simple and Fast (Russ Cox)
notes: https://swtch.com/~rsc/regexp/regexp1.html. NFA/DFA theory. Directly applicable to check-pattern-compliance.js regex performance.
390 links across 31 domains. 16 individually evaluated in content-eval.jsonl. Cross-reference against 72-skill index.
Standard awesome-list format. Low extraction value.
Case study: 486K stars, 462 open issues, 1 commit/90d. What happens when a curated collection grows without retirement/archival. Directly applicable to managing 72+ skills in JASON-OS.

candidate: Build from Scratch Framing for Domain 01
notes: Feynman principle applied to system understanding. Structure JASON-OS Internal Archaeology (Domain 01) as 'how each system was built' narratives rather than 'what exists' inventories.
Community enthusiasm → commercial acquisition → maintenance bottleneck → dormancy. Cross-ref: overlaps with public-apis stagnation entry.
7 shell tutorials (C, Go, Rust). Canonical references if JASON-OS needs custom command dispatch. Defer until architecture solidifies.
390 links in one README.md. Watch for same impulse in EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md, SKILL_INDEX.md — when a single file becomes the dumping ground.
CC0 in README but no LICENSE file. GitHub API can't detect it. Always include machine-readable LICENSE if publishing.
Systematic methodology for wrapping GUI apps as agent-native CLIs. Directly applicable to JASON-OS Domain 02a.
37 SKILL.md files. Flat command references vs sonash workflow definitions. Design space comparison for skill format evolution.
First public Claude Code plugin format reference. Compare against sonash .claude-plugin/ structure.
registry.json (35 CLIs) → static hub → pip install. Agent-discoverable via meta-skill. Model for JASON-OS skill distribution.
Python prompt_toolkit branded REPL. Pattern portable, code Python-specific. Reference for JASON-OS interactive modes.
frozenset-based subprocess arg validation. Similar to existing sonash security patterns.
Auto-generates SKILL.md from CLI introspection. Pattern portable to Node/TS.
Core insight: wrap GUI software systematically for agents. HARNESS.md is the how-to. Foundational for JASON-OS Domain 02a.
Should JASON-OS invoke CLI-Anything harnesses (Python) or generate Node/TS wrappers using same methodology? Critical architecture decision.
28K stars in 29d, 35 harnesses, 0 CI enforcement. Growth outpacing gates. Opposite of build-your-own-x stagnation.
guides/mcp-backend.md. Wrap MCP servers as CLI backends via ClientSession + stdio_client. You have 3 MCP servers. Directly applicable.
guides/skill-generation.md. Introspect Click decorators → Jinja2 → SKILL.md. Could inform /skill-creator automation.
Lightest-weight harness. No local binary (mermaid.ink cloud). Ready to use for agent-driven diagrams.
AI search + structured extraction via Exa API. Alternative/complement for /deep-research searchers.
74 test files, zero CI enforcement. Tests as documentation, not quality gates. Don't regress on pre-commit enforcement.
35 packages, 0 conftest.py, no shared runner. Plan shared testing if JASON-OS distributes skills as packages.
28K stars/29d with no automated enforcement. Same trajectory as celebrity stagnation, faster.
Skills about HOW to remember. Paradigm shift for auto-memory. Read arXiv 2602.02474.
Mine failures → refine skills → propose new. General-purpose self-improvement.
Description/Purpose/When to Use/How to Apply/Constraints. Compare against SKILL.md.
18KB failure classification + mutation prompts. Skill refinement methodology.
Content + context embeddings. Concept portable, code FAISS+PyTorch.
Usage + reward + EMA tracking for skill/tool selection.
8 conversational + 7 embodied. Direct templates for JASON-OS memory operations. Read all before T4.
Activity capture with temporal context. Template for auto-memory enhancement.
Memory insert with duplicate avoidance + quality criteria.
Core theory paper. NOT FETCHED. MUST read before T4 execution.
42KB monolith, zero tests, no version pins. Extract concepts, not code.
Paper companion code. Won't be maintained. Extract knowledge, don't depend.
114-line autonomous research protocol: setup, experiment loop, keep/discard, crash recovery, NEVER STOP, simplicity criterion. Compare against SKILL.md format.
Fixed 5-min time budget makes all experiments comparable. ~12/hour, ~100 overnight. Apply to any time-bounded agent work.
prepare.py (don't touch) + train.py (agent edits) + program.md (human edits). Clean editable-zone contract.
Read stack trace, attempt fix, revert if unfixable. Simpler than checkpoint+resume for low-cost tasks.
commit/metric/memory/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent. Low extraction value.
'Removing something and getting equal or better results is a great outcome.' Growth discipline for 72-skill ecosystem.
program.md (protocol: one agent, one metric, infinite loop) vs SKILL.md (workflow: multi-agent, multi-phase, convergence). When to use which?
NEVER STOP (autoresearch) vs explicit approval gates (CLAUDE.md #2). JASON-OS needs: autonomous within phases, gated between phases.
Framework: load TSV, keep/discard rates, progress plot with running minimum, rank improvements by delta. Apply to review-metrics.jsonl, learning-routes.jsonl.
.gitignore: worktrees/ + queue/ + generated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Multi-agent infrastructure exists but isn't shared. Mirrors your worktree usage.
Karpathy can get away with no license. You can't. Always include LICENSE if publishing JASON-OS.
val_bpb works because the problem has one metric. Don't flatten multi-objective problems into one number.
skill.py reads SKILL.md from package data, stamps version, writes to ~/.claude/skills/. Solves cross_locale_config. Needs Node port.
22 auto-run + 7 ask-first with per-command reasons. Retrofit candidate for add-debt, pr-review, session-end, audit-* skills.
rpc-health.yml: daily cron, 3 exit codes, auto-creates labeled GitHub issues. External contract monitoring pattern.
4-step embedded PR workflow. Alternative to /pr-review. Ambient vs invoked design question.
Literal Task() invocations in SKILL.md workflows. Reduces agent cognitive load.

Lines 94-128. Direct template for adding autonomy rules to sonash skills.
Reference implementation. SkillTarget dataclass, version stamping, drift detection.
Complete 90-line workflow. Daily cron, 3 exit codes, auto-issue creation, artifact upload.
Well-structured CLAUDE.md + ambient PR workflow. Compare against sonash CLAUDE.md v5.9.
Separate file for multi-agent guidance. Consider if CLAUDE.md Section 7 grows.
Entire project wraps obfuscated Google RPC endpoints. Prefer documented APIs.
280 lines for copy file + stamp version. Extract pattern, not complexity.

API/Desc/Auth/HTTPS/CORS with constrained enum values. Reference for JASON-OS skill/tool catalog.
format.py (277 lines) + tests (466 lines). Regex-based format enforcement. Simpler version of patterns:check.
OAuth via Firebase Auth. Direct SoNash integration: sobriety milestones, meeting reminders, daily check-ins.
No auth, CORS yes. Zero-friction weather data for mood-weather correlation journaling.
Same Firebase/Google Cloud ecosystem. Journal sentiment analysis for emotional pattern tracking.
29-line daily cron. Directly transferable for SKILL_INDEX.md, EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md link checking.
Second data point with codecrafters. Proves automation alone doesn't prevent stagnation. MERGED cross-repo finding.
Format validation ensures structural integrity but not content freshness. Applies to patterns:check.
10 promoted APIs before community content. Erodes trust. Keep sponsors separate from content if JASON-OS has partners.

candidate: Three-layer architecture pattern for LLM knowledge bases
notes: Maps to JASON-OS extraction framing. Raw sources / wiki / schema = .research/ / docs+MEMORY / CLAUDE.md+skills.
Ingest=/repo-analysis+/website-analysis, Query=/deep-research+/repo-synthesis, Lint=orphan detection+/alerts+health scripts. Gap: no unified orchestration.
Key gap: /deep-research and /brainstorm outputs archive to .research/ but don't feed back into active knowledge layer. T24 synthesis adoption should close this.
Already have: DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md=index, SESSION_HISTORY.md+commit-log.jsonl=log, research-index.jsonl=research index. Validate wiring between them.
Evaluate for JASON-OS search layer. Currently Grep+index-based. At 1000+ docs may need hybrid BM25/vector search.
Validates brainstorm Phase 1 philosophy. Potential: add minimum-exploration threshold to convergence loops before allowing convergence. Testable.
Claude native <thinking> blocks supersede this. Historical reference only — no action needed.

candidate: Anti-cramming / anti-thinning balance for knowledge skills
notes: When to split vs enrich. Third paragraph about sub-topic = split. Stub with 4 entries mentioning it = enrich. Apply to EXTRACTIONS.md and synthesis output granularity.
Every N items: rebuild indexes, audit quality, check for anti-patterns. Apply to batch synthesis and /deep-research mid-pipeline.
5-agent batches auditing 6 dimensions each. Compare against our parallelization patterns when building T24.
Identity shapes operations: 'what does this mean' vs 'where do I file this'. T24 synthesis adoption should synthesize, not file.

notes: Re-read before edit, never delete without reading, rebuild indices at end. Partially covered by CLAUDE.md #12.

notes: 6 meta tools (search, get-schema, auth, execute, workbench, bash) instead of loading 1000+ definitions. Agent discovers at runtime. Applicable to JASON-OS at 100+ skills.

candidate: Native vs MCP tradeoff framework with token cost data
notes: Native: context control + interception. MCP: simplicity. 55K tokens for 5 MCP servers. Measure current 3-server overhead. Add to AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md.
Standardized llms.txt at site root for LLM consumption. Park for JASON-OS documentation strategy.
Webhook for real-time capable services, polling for those without. Park for event-driven agent features.
moviepy frame extraction + FAISS/OpenSearch similarity dedup. Configurable FPS, 10min chunks. Claims 50% frame reduction. Core pattern for T27.
Three-level temporal decomposition. Each level has summaries, timestamps, metadata. Structures video content analysis.
Each ML feature independently enabled via config. Adding new capabilities = adding new function to pipeline. Extensibility pattern.
Local vector similarity using FAISS. Same dedup quality as OpenSearch, no cloud dependency. Direct port candidate for T27.
Map subtitle segments to frame timestamps. Non-obvious multimodal alignment engineering.
Templates for moderation, summarization, IAB classification against extracted metadata.
FAISS alternative exists in same repo. Don't add search cluster for video dedup when local works.
52 Python files, 1 test. AWS reference architectures skip tests. Don't follow this pattern.
pytubefix download + moviepy audio extraction to MP3. Full lifecycle in ~30 lines. Reference for T27.
Start job -> poll -> fetch transcript -> cleanup. Speaker labels, S3 output routing. T27 speech-to-text reference.
Comprehend PII -> token replacement (T1/T2) -> persist map -> LLM with tokens -> untokenize. Privacy-preserving summarization. Relevant to SoNash privacy-first.

candidate: Chain of Responsibility + Factory handler composition
notes: Reader/processor/writer taxonomy with set_next() chaining and factory auto-discovery. Clean pipeline composition model.
HandlerFactory walks handler tree, parses Python ASTs for AbstractHandler subclasses, registers for lazy import. Zero-config extension.
Env-driven model request/response mapping via JSONPath. Same code for Claude v2, Claude 3, Titan.

1.5K stars, MIT. Built-in SRT caption extraction, playlist/channel enumeration, search API with filters, AsyncYouTube. Caption extraction may eliminate Whisper/Transcribe for captioned videos. T27 workhorse.
Handler demonstrates full async Transcribe lifecycle with speaker diarization.
5+ AWS service deps create vendor lock-in. Prefer local-first (Whisper, Tesseract, spaCy). Repo itself has commented-out Whisper handler.
time.sleep(30) polling loop for Transcribe. Fine for CLI, must not copy into agent pipelines.
Complete local Whisper lifecycle: CUDA detection, large-v3, beam_size=10, vad_filter, segment iteration with tqdm, metadata capture. T27 reference.
beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True prevents hallucination-on-silence. Production tuning.
Cross-repo insight: both bedrock and this repo skip captions. pytubefix has yt.captions. Caption-first for ~80% of videos, Whisper fallback for rest.
asyncio.Semaphore(4) for concurrent downloads, sequential GPU transcription. Network parallelizes, GPU doesn't.
CTranslate2 Whisper. 4x faster than OpenAI whisper. GPU+CPU. THE local transcription engine for T27.
numba.cuda.is_available(), Anaconda toolkit paths, float16 vs auto compute. Real-world GPU setup.
Server-side SpaCy for sentence splitting, client-side compromise.js for paragraph restructuring. Two NLP stages.
$0.006/min API (less accurate) vs free local (more accurate, needs GPU). 1h = $0.36 vs $0.
75KB browser NLP. Sentence/paragraph detection. 11K+ stars.
280 lines, no CLI, no config file. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate. T27 must be modular.
Both bedrock and this repo transcribe every video from scratch. Check captions first (~80% have them).
Module-level variables including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars.

candidate: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
notes: pip install. One-line caption fetch. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage. First adoption recommendation in series.
Undocumented /youtubei/v1/player API with ANDROID client context. No API key. 8 years maintained.
15+ exception types with CAUSE_MESSAGE. RequestBlocked.cause adapts based on proxy config. Gold standard error handling.
Production guide to YouTube IP blocks: proxy config, Webshare residential rotation, retries.
Cross-repo synthesis: L1=youtube-transcript-api (instant/free/~80%), L2=pytubefix captions (backup), L3=faster-whisper (GPU fallback). Clear architecture from 3 repos.
ProxyConfig ABC with Generic + Webshare implementations. Rotating residential IPs, retry-on-429.
find_transcript() checks manual first. Quality hierarchy baked into architecture.
Clean subtitle format generation. SRT (comma) vs WebVTT (dot) separator.
399 commits over 8 years maintaining YouTube API compatibility. Use the library.
YouTube blocks bulk requests. Plan proxy infrastructure from the start.
extractors/<site>/<site>.go pattern. 44 sites, each with own CI workflow. Gold standard for multi-platform content extraction plugins. Go, not Python.
YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Bilibili, Reddit, Vimeo, Twitter/X, Facebook, Douyin, Weibo, Xiaohongshu, VK, and 30+ more. Reference for T27 platform scope expansion beyond YouTube.
46 GitHub Actions workflows — one per supported site. Each tests that site's extractor independently. Remarkable CI pattern for multi-platform tools.

candidate: lux CLI as video acquisition tool for non-YouTube platforms
notes: go install or brew install lux. Downloads video from 44 sites. T27 Layer 0 for non-YouTube platforms where neither transcripts nor captions exist. Feed to Whisper.
